prompt: Create a crisp app icon for the kmsh.dev portfolio. The mark must identify Keshav Mishra and production LLM/ML engineering at favicon scale.
subject: A flat, strong-silhouette KM monogram fused with one small agent-node graph motif. The letters K and M must be readable, simple, and centered.
style: vector-like brand mark, flat but polished, no 3D object, no illustration scene
composition: square icon, centered mark, generous padding, readable at 16px and 64px
palette: dark slate background, cyan brand stroke, violet accent stroke, neutral white highlight
constraints: Render only the exact letters KM once. Strong silhouette. Balanced negative space. No face, no person, no extra letters.
negative: generic network logo, neon glow, fake app badge, unreadable monogram, watermark

prompt: Create the homepage Open Graph image for Keshav Mishra's portfolio. It must communicate the person and role first, then the technical domain.
subject: Left side: clean typographic identity for Keshav Mishra. Right side: one production AI system map showing agent orchestration, evaluation, deployment, and observability as four labeled modules.
style: premium editorial technology social card, shippable portfolio brand asset, not a screenshot
composition: 1920x1200 landscape, strong left text block, right semantic system diagram, high contrast, safe margins for social cropping
palette: dark slate, cyan brand, violet accent, neutral white text, restrained emerald status accent
text: Keshav Mishra; LLM & ML Engineer; Agents; Evaluation; Deploy; Observe
constraints: Render the exact text only. Use 4 technical modules max. Make the role readable in a social preview. No template names.
negative: Magic Portfolio, Selene Yu, fake dashboards, dense neon infographic, unreadable microcopy, stock sci-fi background, watermark

prompt: Create a blog cover for an article about building production-grade LLM agents. The viewer should understand that the article is about reliability and operational controls, not generic AI.
subject: A single agent run lifecycle with five labeled stages: Prompt, Tools, Validate, Fallback, Monitor. Include one small incident/risk signal and one resolved status signal.
style: clean technical editorial diagram, dark-mode website cover, not a dashboard screenshot
composition: 2048x1152 16:9 cover, one horizontal flow, readable labels, clear hierarchy, enough edge margin for card crop
palette: dark slate, cyan connectors, violet model core, neutral text, restrained amber warning and emerald success
constraints: Render the exact five labels only. Each label must map to one visible stage. Keep it sparse and explanatory.
negative: brain icon centerpiece, random robot, fake code text, many unlabeled panels, neon clutter, watermark

prompt: Create a case-study cover for Genie, an LLM-driven code generation and agent scaffolding platform. The viewer should understand natural language becomes tested deployable agent code.
subject: A concise 5-step product workflow: Brief -> Plan -> Generate -> Test -> Ship. Show input brief, planning graph, generated code file, validation gate, and deploy package.
style: high-end product workflow diagram with Once UI-like dark surfaces and precise spacing
composition: 2048x1152 16:9 cover, five modules max, left-to-right flow, one central generated-code module, readable labels at card size
palette: dark slate, cyan brand, violet accent, neutral white text, muted emerald validation
constraints: Render the exact five labels only. No fake code paragraphs. The image must explain the product workflow in one glance.
negative: overstuffed architecture chart, illegible code, random LLM brain, rocket launch cliche, watermark

prompt: Create a case-study cover for Text2SQL2Plot, an industrial analytics agent. The viewer should understand field questions become safe SQL and operational charts.
subject: A compact 4-step flow: Ask -> SQL -> Validate -> Plot. Include a turbine/sensor silhouette as the domain anchor and one clean time-series chart as the output.
style: industrial technical diagram, premium dark UI editorial cover, not a generic dashboard
composition: 2048x1152 16:9 cover, turbine/sensor anchor on one side, four labeled stages, output chart as final focal point
palette: dark slate, cyan data lines, violet SQL/model accent, neutral text, restrained amber validation accent
constraints: Render the exact four labels only. Make the turbine/sensor context visible but secondary. Keep modules sparse.
negative: fake SQL query text, tiny unreadable labels, refinery stock art, dense dashboard, watermark

prompt: Create a case-study cover for an oil and gas RAG chatbot. The viewer should understand technical documents become cited answers through retrieval.
subject: A 4-step retrieval pipeline: Docs -> Embed -> Retrieve -> Cite. Include document stack, vector index, ranked chunks, and cited answer card.
style: clean knowledge-retrieval diagram with dark slate Once UI surfaces, precise spacing, practical product feel
composition: 2048x1152 16:9 cover, four labeled modules max, one clear retrieval core, no background scene clutter
palette: dark slate, cyan retrieval lines, violet vector index, neutral text, restrained amber citation markers
constraints: Render the exact four labels only. The citation concept must be visually explicit with numbered source markers.
negative: oil-rig cliche, generic chatbot bubble only, fake paragraphs, many tiny documents, watermark

prompt: Create a case-study cover for an end-to-end AutoML and MLOps platform. The viewer should understand model lifecycle automation and monitoring.
subject: A concise lifecycle loop with five labels: Data -> Train -> Registry -> Deploy -> Monitor. Include drift detection as one small warning state tied to Monitor.
style: clean model lifecycle diagram, premium dark product surface, practical MLOps asset
composition: 2048x1152 16:9 cover, circular or left-to-right lifecycle, five modules max, one chart surface, readable labels
palette: dark slate, cyan flow, violet model registry, emerald healthy state, restrained amber drift warning
constraints: Render the exact five labels only. The lifecycle should be obvious without surrounding copy. Keep it sparse.
negative: generic ML dashboard, fake metric text, too many model icons, neon poster, watermark
